name: Bai Linay
text: Stay a while and listen, James. Our people, the T'boli, have lived in harmony with these lands for generations.

name: Bai Linay
text: You see the T'nalak cloth I am holding? This is more than just fabric. It is the soul of South Cotabato.

name: Bai Linay
text: We weave it from the finest abaca fibers, using dyes we find in the forest.

name: Bai Linay
text: People ask where we get our designs. We do not use patterns or sketches.

name: Bai Linay
text: We receive them as visions in our dreams, a gift from Fu Dalu, the Spirit of the Abaca.

name: Bai Linay
text: This is why the world knows us as the Dreamweavers.

name: Bai Linay
text: This sacred bond is strongest here at Lake Sebu.

name: Bai Linay
text: But respect the water, Archivist. Our geography makes us vulnerable to Flash Floods.

name: Bai Linay
text: Our elders taught us that when the floods come, you do not stand and watch.

name: Bai Linay
text: You must run to the higher ground. Only those who respect the earth can survive its power.

name: James
text: Thank you, Bai Linay. I will remember your words.
